text:
Priya Desai: Quick sync — we have a customer asking for an end-to-end pilot of FastRoute in ~2 weeks. Main blockers: infra prewarm (reserved capacity), runtime quantization/profile, and KMS/HSM key flow. I think we should decide staged scope today. TL;DR: staging-only pilot first, HSM later. Thoughts?
Alex Chen: Staging-only sounds safest. Runtime can cut a quantization profile and a conservative batching default, but 2 weeks is tight. Can we limit telemetry and non-blocking features? :thinking_face:
Samir Patel: Capacity note: to guarantee latency SLOs we need to reserve a small GPU pool and prewarm it for the pilot window. That requires a capacity request and cost sign-off. 2 weeks -> we need the request by EOD to make it.
Jordan Blake: Security: HSM/KMS review + customer key ownership is non-trivial. We can run without HSM in staging if the customer accepts envelope keys, but produ...

text:
Asha Reddy: Quick pulse 99 we need a compact DP run to validate Private onboarding and our replay harness. Goal: 6 partners, 3-week staggered starts, 21-45 day pilots depending on complexity.

Ben Carter: +1. Keep screener minimal: use case, compliance (HIPAA/SOC2/PCI), expected QPS.

Ivy Cho: SLOs: gen p50 < 80ms, p95 < 300ms @256 tokens. embeddings p50 < 30ms, p95 < 80ms. Separate buckets.

Samir Khan: Success gates: 1) E2E integration + smoke tests 2) perf SLOs met in replay run 3) 6 labeled failure cases submitted 4) partner agrees to feedback cadence.

Asha Reddy: Incentives: 2 months discounted Dedicated credits + up to 3 engineering days. Offer co-marketing for strategic partners.

Ivy Cho: Tooling we'll provide: replay harness (private repo), token sensor script, eval CSV template. Partners must run smoke tests in staging for 72h.

Ben Carter: Legal needs anonymized metrics co...

text:
Alex Kim: Q2 flightcheck — short status for OKR milestone owners. Reply with one-liners: shipped / slipped / next-step. Please include any blocking deps or PRs that need reviews. Aiming to lock the OKR doc by EOD Friday. :airplane:

Maria Gonzales: runtime-v3 autoscale — shipped ✅ Canary at 50% for web tier. No major regressions so far. PR: https://github.com/redwood/runtime/pull/421 needs review (mainly infra test changes). Next: roll to 100% after Chen reviews.

Chen Li: I'll review PR421 this afternoon. Heads-up: one e2e test flakes on Dedicated env — that's the slipped bit. Blocking dep: eng-infra needs the new GPU image (ticket #PLAT-87). Next-step: fix flake + coordinate image bump with infra.

Samir Patel: console telemetry retention feature — slipped. Storage budget exceeded during load test. Short-term mitigation: retention toggle + backfill compaction job. PR: https://github...

title: #product: 5567890123

Nina Park: Heads up 1: we have two regulated pilot customers asking for SLA exceptions (longer data residency commitments + legal indemnities). Need a quick alignment: what parts of GA gating do we relax vs what must stay for public GA? @Raj @Omar @Lena @Ben @Emma

Raj Singh: Short answer: legal can approve limited ...
text:
Nina Park: Heads up 1: we have two regulated pilot customers asking for SLA exceptions (longer data residency commitments + legal indemnities). Need a quick alignment: what parts of GA gating do we relax vs what must stay for public GA? @Raj @Omar @Lena @Ben @Emma

Raj Singh: Short answer: legal can approve limited SLA exceptions for named accounts but only if security controls (VPC, KMS) and audit logs are in place. We need clear scope and a time-limited clause (e.g., 6 months) and commercial signoff. Also require change to TOS addendum.

Omar Reyes: Security checklist for exceptions: 1) Dedicated or Private deployment, 2) Customer-managed KMS, 3) Audit log retention >= 1 year, 4) Pen-test within 90 days, 5) Network allowlist. If any of these are missing, we cannot relax data residency.

Lena Mfcller: Sales note: both customers are willing to sign a pilot addendum and pay for extra s...

text:
Evan Park: Quick thread 99need alignment on two catalog items for next sprint: (1) 'cerberus-lite-1' onboarding as a fast-path small model, (2) staged deprecation timeline for 'falcon-old-v2' pinned customers. High level proposal in the next message.

Diego Ruiz: Curious about target customers for cerberus-lite-1. Is this for low-latency mobile use cases or just cost-sensitive tenants?
Priya Natarajan: Primary use case = latency-sensitive mobile + cheap embedding fallback. We should classify as "tier B" model with opt-in pin.
Ben Cho: Ops note: cerberus-lite-1 requires different quantization runtime. Need infra sign-off. Can reserve a 1-GPU test pool.
Evan Park: Proposal bullets:
- Cerberus-lite-1 onboarding: 7-day quick canary (hosted only), bench targets: p95 <= 90ms, regressions <=3% vs baseline on mobile prompts, token-cost <= -12% ...

text:
Alex Kim: Heads-up — ACME pilot asked explicitly for the same response formats as Competitor X (CSV + per-field confidence). They're pushing for parity on output schema. We need to decide whether to scope a parity workstream for GA or defer to a later add-on.
Priya Rao: Is this blocking the pilot launch or just a nice-to-have?
Alex Kim: Pilot is green for a narrow MVP, but ACME says their integration tests fail without the CSV export. They could delay if we can't ship a small adapter.
Ben Carter: Quick question: can we hack an export adapter in Console > Exports with 2 engineer-weeks?
Marta Lopez: We could, but we should check productization cost (support, docs, testing). If it's a one-off adapter, ops burden might be high.
Priya Rao: Proposed options: 
1) Ship an adapter in Dedicated as a short-term patch (P0 for pilot).
2) Add a generic output-shaping feature to Redwood API (P1 for ...

text:
Tara Nguyen: quick sync 1 post-launch pulse: we have 2 P0s (signup race, billing double-charge), 9 low-ux bugs, and 3 customer enhancement asks. can we agree on a compact plan?
Ibrahim Khan: my take 1 run a 4-day bug sprint with a tiny "invest" lane (1 engineer) for a gated enhancement. keeps momentum without spreading focus.
Sofia Martin: +1. Need clear acceptance criteria for the enhancements before they enter the invest lane. otherwise they balloon.
Ethan Park: ops note 1 signup race looks like a distributed lock gap under burst load. i can push a mitigation toggle that reduces contention; needs a canary and a short DB migration window.
Tara Nguyen: proposal:
- Mon-Thu: stability sprint (hotfix lane for customer-tied regressions)
- Fri: ship one behind-flag enhancement (if safety gates pass) + retro
Gates to close sprint: no unresolved P0s, smoke tests green, p99 within baseline *1...

text:
Ava Chen: Quick design review for the autoscale onboarding modal. Main asks: SLO slider default, min/max labels, burst wording, and guardrail copy.
Liam Patel: The current SLO default (92%) boosts min unexpectedly for new users. Recommend soft default 70% and explain with short helper.
Sofi Gomez: a11y: pool selector still traps focus after close on VoiceOver. Make it role=dialog and ensure focus returns to trigger. Add aria-live for the cost delta.
Marco Rivera: Users think "burst" is permanent. Two options: 'Burst (temporary)' label OR add inline helper: 'Short-term extra GPUs — auto-removes.' Also tooltip about billing.
Ellie Tran: Fallback messaging needs to state routing and perf impact. Draft toast: "Fallback active: selected pool at capacity. Routing to Redwood Hosted for availability; expect higher tail latency. Data encrypted in transit. (Details)"
Ava Chen: Love that. Also p...

text:
Lina Torres: Quick sync—drafting the pilot outreach and acceptance criteria for our Private preview. Need decisions on gating, incentives, and what success looks like.
Ethan Park: Prefer we lock minimal security gates first (KMS or equivalent + documented network policy). Anything less and infra teams will push back.
Noah Reed: Agreed. Also suggest a lightweight compliance checklist: data residency, DPA required, and whether partner needs air-gapped install.
Asha Rao: For outreach messaging, we should highlight influence on product roadmap and concierge onboarding to increase conversion.
Lina Torres: Proposed gating checklist (working):
- Security: KMS/HSM or managed key policy in place
- Network: VPC peering or documented firewall rules
- Legal: pilot NDA & DPA baseline approved
- Ops: contact point for infra + rough QPS estimate

text:
Ava Chen: ok team — proposing a soft-launch for the design partner (DP) cohort next Wed. want to lock acceptance criteria & recruiting pitch.

Mike Rivera: sounds good. what's target size? 8-12?

Sophia Lee: prefer 6 active partners first week to iterate quickly. propose 2-week trial period so we can validate onboarding and feedback loops.

Jordan Park: agree on smaller. acceptance tests should be product + infra checklist. returning items we need to measure: onboarding time <1h, integration sample up in 3 calls, error rate <1%.

Ava Chen: capturing that. draft acceptance checklist:
```
- NDA signed
- onboarding doc completed (first request -> sample request)
- integration test passes (sample request -> expected response)
- latency p50 <200ms for sample workload
- telemetry events flowing and traceable to partner ID
- smoke regression run (no critical failures)
```

Emma Davis: for re...

title: #product: 2078901234
preview: Ava Chen: Quick intake — customer (FinSage) ran a canary and saw weird regressions when we routed to quantized 8-bit variants. Problem statement: some tenants observed answer hallucinations and formatting drops only on the canary cohort. Need to capture goals, non-goals, and concrete success metrics for a PRD so inf...
text:
Ava Chen: Quick intake — customer (FinSage) ran a canary and saw weird regressions when we routed to quantized 8-bit variants. Problem statement: some tenants observed answer hallucinations and formatting drops only on the canary cohort. Need to capture goals, non-goals, and concrete success metrics for a PRD so infra/product can scope rollout controls. @Samir Shah @Priya Nair can you jump in? TL;DR proposal: per-tenant canary + auto-rollback on eval regressions + manual override in console.

Samir Shah: Thanks—quick Q: do we have a repro prompt set from the customer or is this flaky across many prompts? Also what was the traffic % for the canary?

Ava Chen: They shared ~120 failure examples but it's noisy. Canary was 5% traffic for 2h, and errors spiked after a model compact/quantize push. We should treat this as a plausible production regression vector.

Priya Nair: Proposed goals /...

title: #product: 2048001234
preview:

Liam O'Connor: +1. we should be expli...
text:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — we need to align on scope & launch criteria for the next observability push: dashboards, tracing hooks, per-tenant cost breakdown, error analytics. Customer asks (AcmeHealth + two mid-market pilots) are pushing for cost-by-route and span-level annotations.
Liam O'Connor: +1. we should be explicit about sampling and retention tradeoffs. full trace retention for every request will blow costs.
Priya Sharma: Agree. Suggested approach: default adaptive sampling + an opt-in full-trace bucket for enterprise customers.
Mateo Ruiz: From SEs: AcmeHealth needs per-tenant cost in billing UI and a CSV export. They said seeing token-level cost by customer is a must for procurement.
Ava Chen: Good note. Proposal MVP: 1) dashboard templates (latency, error rate, cost summary) 2) tracing hooks SDK (span annotations + service tags) 3) cost-by-tenant in console w/ CSV export 4) ou...

text:
Olivia Chen: Heads-up 1: quick review of the compact catalog rows — we're adding a density toggle and the performance badges + version pin behaviors need final UX/a11y sign-off. Figma: https://www.figma.com/file/abcd1234 (compact-rows)
Ravi Menon: updated the compact variant this morning. Two problems I hit: 1) pin icon overlaps the badge on narrow widths, 2) when a version is pinned the row expands and pushes other rows down.
Zoe Kim: on keyboard nav the expanded metadata gets focused before the pin control which is confusing. In compact mode we should keep a single tab stop per row unless the user expands deliberately.
Priya Singh: Accessibility: badges currently announce as "badge" to screen readers. We need a semantic readout: metric name, value, unit, and pass/fail status. Example aria-label: "Throughput 120 rps; p95 latency 18 ms; meets SLO."

text:
Maya Chen (PM): Quick sync — proposing a lightweight set of onboarding + lifecycle gates for the catalog. Goal: reduce customer surprises and shorten time-to-serve. TL;DR: acceptance tests, perf thresholds, rollout gates, notice windows, fallback variants, and a 30d \"gracepath\" for deprecated models. Thoughts?

Devon Li (Eng): +1. Do we mean lab benchmarks or production e2e? We should separate bench vs prod SLOs.

Priya Shah (ML Eng): My take: two tiered thresholds: bench (evals + unit perf) and production (latency p95, cost per tok, regression on eval suite). Bench pass required to be listed; prod pass required for 1st-week canary.

Ben Carter (Product Eng): Need customer impact matrix for breaking changes. Suggest categories: Minor (quantization change, same API), Major (behavioral regressions), Shutdown (sunset). Each ticket has required comms and migration paths.

Omar Ruiz (Sup...

text:
Nina Flores: Sharing a compact iteration of the Private/VPC setup wizard networking step. Goals: reduce cognitive load, make remediation actionable, and improve accessibility. Figma link: https://www.figma.com/file/ijkl9012. Can we surface quick fixes for non-network folks?

Ethan Park: Nice. Two quick UX asks: 1) show a short summary line under the stepper like '1 critical failure, 2 advisories' so users know severity at a glance; 2) rename the main CTA to 'Verify network health' (longer but clearer).

Lina Gomez: From infra: preflight probes we run: DNS resolution, NAT egress, TLS handshake, port reachability, and model endpoint ping. Some probes can hang ~30s; we should show live streaming of results and let users cancel the run. Per-probe retry is useful.

text:
Aisha Khan: quick design Q — invoice download affordance on the compact spend canvas. thinking about org/project toggle + where the download CTA lives. screenshot: https://redwood.app/ux/shot/1854321000.png
Ben Carter: my instinct: keep primary CTA at the row. accountants expect per-invoice export right where the line is. toolbar-level download felt ambiguous to me. :thinking_face:
Eva Li: Accessibility point: if we keep a row button, it needs a clear aria-label and an accessible name with scope (org/project) and billing period. also ensure keyboard target meets size guidelines.
Samir Patel: Implementation note — per-row buttons blow up the DOM in the virtualized list. We can make a delegated action (one listener, data-row-id) to avoid hydration/SSR issues. Perf matters here.
Aisha Khan: What if we do a hybrid pattern: row-level inline affordance (minimal icon) + a sticky selection to...

title: #product: 1834567890
preview:
Maya: Quick design review — I ran through the new key creation stepper in the console. Step 2 (permissions) is easy to skip because it's visually the same as metadata. users are missing the scope toggles.
Sam: Saw that in user testing too. They hit Next then complain the key can't do X. Can we make the scopes sectio...
text:
Maya: Quick design review — I ran through the new key creation stepper in the console. Step 2 (permissions) is easy to skip because it's visually the same as metadata. users are missing the scope toggles.
Sam: Saw that in user testing too. They hit Next then complain the key can't do X. Can we make the scopes section more prominent? maybe a bordered box + short hint 'Who can use this key?'
Olivia: Accessibility note: the 'this key will not be shown again' text is not read by screen readers. It's plain text below the input — add aria-describedby on the token field and make the notice aria-live=polite when created.
Jordan: Copy question — should the primary CTA be 'Create key' or 'Create and copy key'? My vote: explicit 'Create key' then show the token block with a clear Copy button. auto-copy feels surprising.
Maya: +1 for not auto-copying. Also the token display should have a short TT...

title: #product: 1826004821
preview:
Ava Chen: quick design check — iterating on the playground chat UI. main goals: streaming display clarity, a single stop/regenerate affordance, token counter placement, and prompt-library preview. dropping a figma link in a sec. thoughts?
Jasper: +1 on consolidating stop/regenerate. do we show both states as one but...
text:
Ava Chen: quick design check — iterating on the playground chat UI. main goals: streaming display clarity, a single stop/regenerate affordance, token counter placement, and prompt-library preview. dropping a figma link in a sec. thoughts?
Jasper: +1 on consolidating stop/regenerate. do we show both states as one button with label swap, or two separate actions?
Elaine M: prefer one dynamic primary button. when streaming -> label `Stop`, after finish -> `Regenerate`. reduces visual clutter for keyboard users.
Tom R: accessibility: ensure the streaming area uses `role=status`/`aria-live` so screen readers hear token arrival. also keyboard support for Stop (Enter) while focused.
Riya Patel: tiny copy nit: on mobile `Stop` can be ambiguous. propose `Stop generation` for small widths, desktop stays `Stop`.
Jasper: re token counter — where to surface it? inline in the input, floating top-rig...

title: #product: 1809998887
preview:
Leah (PM): Quick sync — thinking we should formalize 'platform ladders' as the prioritization + acceptance rubric for routing/multi-region features. Keep convo short: what tiers, gating metrics, and customer ask mapping?
Ava Chen: +1. Proposal: three ladders — Pilot (beta customers), Scale (dedicated customers), Har...
text:
Leah (PM): Quick sync — thinking we should formalize 'platform ladders' as the prioritization + acceptance rubric for routing/multi-region features. Keep convo short: what tiers, gating metrics, and customer ask mapping?
Ava Chen: +1. Proposal: three ladders — Pilot (beta customers), Scale (dedicated customers), Harden (SLA + enterprise). Each needs clear latency p50/p95, cost delta, and isolation guarantees.
Mason Patel: Agree. For latency: Pilot p95 <= 250ms, Scale p95 <= 180ms, Harden p95 <= 120ms. Cost: relative token cost delta vs baseline < 25/15/5% respectively.
Priya Singh: For isolation, Pilot = soft tenant fencing (logical), Scale = tenant pools + quotas, Harden = dedicated slices + region pinning. Also need evict policy and runbooks.
Leah (PM): Good. Customer asks: fintech/healthcare require Harden. Start mapping active customers to ladders and call out migration runway.
Sa...

title: #product: 1799998000
preview:

Marcus Lee: +1 saw that. repro: invite a user while SSO enforced on t...
text:
Ava Chen: quick design flag — when org has SSO enforced, the invite progress toast still shows a generic 'Invite sent' state. users expect an explicit 'SSO enforced — link sent to IdP' or similar. also the toast disappears too fast :thinking_face:
Marcus Lee: +1 saw that. repro: invite a user while SSO enforced on the org, the invite flow redirects to the idp, but the toast doesn't mention SSO. can we surface an affordance to view next steps? :eyes:
Priya Khatri: UX note — if we add extra copy it needs to be short and actionable for keyboard/screen-reader users. suggestion: 'SSO enforced — user must accept via corporate SSO. Open org SSO settings' with an inline link. also color contrast for the SSO badge is low on the current teal background.
Sam Torres: dev note — the current toast component auto-dismiss is 3s. for SSO states I think it should be persistent until dismissed or link c...

title: #product: 1798765432
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — I prototyped a flow where min/max are inline steppers and burst is a compact pill. Noticed tab order jumps when toggling chain icon. Thoughts?
Samir Patel: can you attach the prototype link? also looks fine on wide screens but mobile stack might be weird.
Ava Chen: https://figma.com/proto/xyz ...
text:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — I prototyped a flow where min/max are inline steppers and burst is a compact pill. Noticed tab order jumps when toggling chain icon. Thoughts?
Samir Patel: can you attach the prototype link? also looks fine on wide screens but mobile stack might be weird.
Ava Chen: https://figma.com/proto/xyz (mock) — try the keyboard flow: Tab -> min stepper -> max stepper -> chain toggle -> burst pill.
Tomás Ruiz: Reproduced. When chain is ON, focus moves from max -> chain -> burst. When OFF, chain is visually present but removed from tab order which causes focus to skip back to top of the form. Feels jarring.
Priya Nair: Suggestion: keep chain toggle in tab order but mark aria-pressed="true/false" and aria-describedby to include the linked behavior. That avoids focus jumps and maintains semantics.
QA Bot: a11y check: burst pill color contrast (disabled state) is 2.7:1 — below...

title: #product: 1787654322
preview:
Sofia Alvarez: quick pass on the compact routing editor mock — focus areas: reorder affordance, validation visibility, and sim preview noise. figma: https://www.figma.com/file/compact-ux

Ben Carter: looks good overall. two quick nits: the drag handle hit area feels tiny and the reorder animation causes the preview ...
text:
Sofia Alvarez: quick pass on the compact routing editor mock — focus areas: reorder affordance, validation visibility, and sim preview noise. figma: https://www.figma.com/file/compact-ux

Ben Carter: looks good overall. two quick nits: the drag handle hit area feels tiny and the reorder animation causes the preview snapshot to flicker. could we make the handle full-row height and animate transforms only? :eyes:

Ivy Park: +1 on handle. also suggest a small visual cue when a rule is 'picked up' for keyboard users (outline + aria-live). keyboard proposal: Space to pick up, Up/Down to move, Space to drop. aria-live strings: "Picked up rule 'EU mobile'", "Moved 'EU mobile' to position 2."

Marco Silva: validation copy is terse: current "invalid matcher" -> prefer "Matcher syntax error 12 expected: equals, contains, regex. Click for examples." show this inline under the rule but collapsed ...

title: #product: 1778889000
preview:
Marta Ruiz: Short recap from today's routing-patterns workshop. We focused on making a productized set of 'residency dials' for customers: how to express residency/latency/cost preferences and what we will guarantee. Keep replies concise and nominate owners.
Liam O'Connor: Love the dials metaphor. Proposal: three co...
text:
Marta Ruiz: Short recap from today's routing-patterns workshop. We focused on making a productized set of 'residency dials' for customers: how to express residency/latency/cost preferences and what we will guarantee. Keep replies concise and nominate owners.
Liam O'Connor: Love the dials metaphor. Proposal: three core templates (latency-priority, cost-shared, residency-strong). Each has a fallback behavior and a pricing band.
Priya Nair: Implementation reality check: templates must map to concrete routing rules + scheduler hints + model-variant pins (e.g., int8 vs fp16). Document a sample mapping per template.
Ethan Park: Ops ask: define headroom and evacuation signals per region. If headroom < 7% trigger evacuation flow. Need a clear default: soft residency -> spill; hard residency -> reject + page.
Sofia Gomez: Sales input: customers want expected lead time and a one-line descriptio...

title: #product: 1772032111

Specifically: (1) confidence threshold to call something a known issue vs “investigating”, (2) workaround validation bar, (3) who owns updates/expiry. Draft is in PM-913: https://linear.app/redw...
text:
Chris Osei: Need a decision today on the publication criteria for “Known Issues” so we can lock PM-913 + unblock Docs PRs. Specifically: (1) confidence threshold to call something a known issue vs “investigating”, (2) workaround validation bar, (3) who owns updates/expiry. Draft is in PM-913: https://linear.app/redwood/issue/PM-913

Dev Patel: +1. Support keeps getting stuck in limbo where we *know* it’s real but Engineering doesn’t want us to publish until there’s an RCA. We need a middle ground.

Elliot Price: From docs POV: we can publish without RCA, but we need clear labeling and update responsibilities. Also we need to avoid anything customer-identifying. (We’ve had drafts with account IDs / exact tenant names—those can’t ship.)

Rachel Kim: Enterprise angle: customers don’t care if we have RCA yet, they care if the workaround is safe + the comms are consistent. But we can’t pub...

title: #product: 1772001234
preview:
Marta Ruiz: Quick sync — product decision needed on convo API shape. Customer asks: some want simple `prompt` param, others expect `messages` array with roles (system/user/assistant). We need launch criteria and scope. Thoughts?
Mike Chen: my 2c: canonical API should be `messages` array. It's explicit, maps to chat ...
text:
Marta Ruiz: Quick sync — product decision needed on convo API shape. Customer asks: some want simple `prompt` param, others expect `messages` array with roles (system/user/assistant). We need launch criteria and scope. Thoughts?
Mike Chen: my 2c: canonical API should be `messages` array. It's explicit, maps to chat models and tool calling. Keep a `prompt` alias in SDKs for ease but server canonicalizes to `messages`.
Priya Patel: Agree. Engineering concerns: telemetry for role-level token counts, model routing decisions, and legacy clients that send a single string. We'll need automatic conversion + warn header. Also impacts batching.
Jordan Blake: Devex ask: docs + migration guides first-class. Example snippets, quickstart & playground support. Also update TS SDK types and python.
Samira Ortiz: Role semantics matter — system should be immutable and high-trust. Recommend server-side e...

title: #product: 1771698421
preview:
Marissa Cole: starting a thread to lock scope + success metrics for Suggestions beta in Console. we need alignment w/ Optimize team this week.

Marissa Cole: proposed beta scope = batching + caching suggestions only. UI = list + details drawer w/ “why”, estimated savings ($/day + %), latency deltas p50/p95, and risk...
text:
Marissa Cole: starting a thread to lock scope + success metrics for Suggestions beta in Console. we need alignment w/ Optimize team this week.

Marissa Cole: proposed beta scope = batching + caching suggestions only. UI = list + details drawer w/ “why”, estimated savings ($/day + %), latency deltas p50/p95, and risk label (low/med/high) w/ explanation + guardrails.

Aditya Rao: +1 on keeping it tight. please explicitly call out non-goals: quantization recs UI, auto-apply, and anything that reads like “guaranteed savings”.

Tessa Morgan: design can support the above. but if we include “model/quantization” in the nav or empty states, it will confuse people. suggest we reference it as “coming soon” only in docs, not in-product.

Dylan Brooks: eng-wise, we can ship this behind flag w/ suggestions API paging/sorting. agree that adding quantization to the view will blow up surface area + pe...

title: #product: 1770721205
preview: Paula Mendes: Hey all — need alignment on canary-based rollout gates for runtime releases. Specifically what we mean by “auto-pause” in the Rollouts UX + release controller. Product expectation is: if perf-canary detects regression on a top model, rollout should stop automatically, with clear reason + next steps. Ca...
text:
Paula Mendes: Hey all — need alignment on canary-based rollout gates for runtime releases. Specifically what we mean by “auto-pause” in the Rollouts UX + release controller. Product expectation is: if perf-canary detects regression on a top model, rollout should stop automatically, with clear reason + next steps. Can we define exact semantics?

Marissa Cole: +1. We’ve been using “auto-pause” loosely in docs and it’s confusing. We should be explicit: pause future promotions vs rollback current. Also: which alert level gates? warning vs paging?

Connor O'Brien: From release-eng side: easiest/safest first cut is “pause promotions” only. i.e., release-controller holds at current stage (no new regions/tier expansions) until manual ack/override. Auto-rollback is a separate feature flag (and higher blast radius).

Noah Patel: +1. Canary signal is probabilistic + we’ve had baseline drift issu...

text:
Chris Osei: flagging a caveat for the Q1 pricing exp readout: meter fixes overlap w/ the experiment window. we need to be explicit in the doc + in the charts or we’re going to argue about conversion/margin deltas that are just metering.

Chris Osei: can we align on the segmentation rule? i’m proposing: pre-meter-fix vs post-meter-fix slices, plus exclude a small rollout buffer window.

Nikhil Sharma: yep. Variant B started 2026-01-22 for self-serve new signups. Meter changes started landing 2/06-ish (retry double-count fix + usage event idempotency tightening + region dimension backfill).

Nikhil Sharma: If we aggregate all of Jan+Feb we’re mixing apples/oranges.

Sana Farid: +1. From stats side, mixing measurement changes into outcome metrics breaks the causal story. Need a clean “measurement regime” dimension.

Sana Farid: Suggest we do:
- Primary analysis: only post-meter-fix (stab...

title: #product: 1770132280
preview: Chris Osei: Heads up: the Variant B pricing experiment is getting *really* noisy after this week’s meter fixes. We need a decision today: stop vs continue (or pause enrollment) so we don’t publish a misleading readout. Current dashboard is showing a step-change in billed_tokens on Feb 12–13 and conversion looks like...
text:
Chris Osei: Heads up: the Variant B pricing experiment is getting *really* noisy after this week’s meter fixes. We need a decision today: stop vs continue (or pause enrollment) so we don’t publish a misleading readout. Current dashboard is showing a step-change in billed_tokens on Feb 12–13 and conversion looks like it “improved” overnight.

Nikhil Sharma: Yeah, I’m looking at it now. The step-change lines up exactly with the usage event idempotency + streaming retry fix rollout. It’s not a user behavior change.

Sana Farid: +1. If the measurement changed mid-flight, the causal story is broken unless we segment pre/post and re-baseline. Otherwise the lift is basically “we stopped double counting.”

Rishi Malhotra: From Finance lens, we can’t use this to justify packaging decisions if the unit economics inputs moved mid-experiment. Margin model uses billed_tokens and cache_hit attribut...

text:
Chris Osei: rolling up a rollout checklist for pricing experiment Variant B. want to run it by folks before we flip anything. can we confirm: eligibility, flags, dashboards, comms?

Nikhil Sharma: yes pls. Variant B is the “new free tier + overage guardrail copy” right? + tweak to overage rate for self-serve?

Chris Osei: yep. Variant A was copy-only, Variant B is copy + packaging thresholds (included usage + overage behavior).

Sana Farid: reminder: if meters changed last week, we need explicit pre/post segmentation in the analysis. for rollout checklist: record meter_version and experiment cohort on every request.

Logan Wright: +1. from telemetry side we can tag `meter_version` + `pricing_experiment_variant`. PR-27533 adds cohort tagging but we need to ensure it’s wired on OpenAI-compat + SDK retries.

Monica Patel: SDK side: python/ts will pass idempotency keys by default on retri...

title: #product: 1769954321
preview:
Alex Chen: Quick sync — we need a concrete spec for an introspection/discovery endpoint that lists runtime-supported features per model instance (tool schemas, streaming modes, token caps). Customer asks from FinReg want deterministic max token limits and function-calling schemas.
Priya Nair: +1. Proposal: /v1/model...
text:
Alex Chen: Quick sync — we need a concrete spec for an introspection/discovery endpoint that lists runtime-supported features per model instance (tool schemas, streaming modes, token caps). Customer asks from FinReg want deterministic max token limits and function-calling schemas.
Priya Nair: +1. Proposal: /v1/models/:id/discover returning compact JSON with keys: supported_tools, streaming_modes, max_input_tokens, max_output_tokens, structured_output_schema, function_schemas, version, compatibility. Keep it stable.
Sam Ortiz: Keep payload small and flag-driven. Instead of heavy nested objects, expose top-level capability flags: supports_functions, supports_streaming, streaming_type, supports_json_output, supports_rerank. Put full schemas behind separate endpoints if needed.
Jordan Blake: Also include quantization/embedding caveats — e.g., "embedding_precision": "fp16|int8|approx" to s...

Two open decisions I want eyes on: 1) launch criteria for Redwood Dedicated SLAs and 2) priority/scope for Redwood Optimize (customer requests + cost tooling). TL;DR proposal: narrow Optimize to beta customers to k...
text:
Asha Patel: Quick pass — I posted the company OKR draft in Drive. Need a final sign-off by EOD Friday. Two open decisions I want eyes on: 1) launch criteria for Redwood Dedicated SLAs and 2) priority/scope for Redwood Optimize (customer requests + cost tooling). TL;DR proposal: narrow Optimize to beta customers to keep timeline, and set a realistic Dedicated latency target. Please chime.
Tom Reynolds: Saw it — thanks. I agree we should trim Optimize scope. One ask: the Dedicated latency SLO in the doc is 50ms p95 for Q3; that feels optimistic. Suggest we move the committed target to 75ms p95 and keep 50ms as a stretch.
Marta Liu: concur on scope trim. For the SLA bump: we can meet 75ms p95 if we do two infra tasks first — KV cache tuning and kernel fallback paths. Rough ETA ~6 weeks of focused infra work. Hitting 50ms p95 would need an additional senior infra hire or deprioritizing an...

text:
Chris Osei: Need to lock go/no-go criteria for marketplace refresh launch + proposed date. Dropping a draft checklist here — please poke holes.

Target launch: Wed 2026-02-18 10am PT (AWS + GCP listings + docs live + console entrypoint enabled)

GO criteria:
1) Docs: marketplace onboarding pages published + nav/redirects in place (no dead links from AWS/GCP listing)
2) Security packet: marketplace security overview + data handling/retention + audit logs pages reviewed/approved (wording consistent)
3) Entitlements: entitlement sync job reliability in prod (p95 entitlement availability < 15 min, failure rate < 1% rolling 24h) + alerting wired
4) Console onboarding: marketplace entrypoint shipped + error states for delayed entitlements + retry guidance
5) Metering: dimension mapping verified end-to-end (AWS + GCP) in staging with a test subscription; no known mismatch bugs
6) Approvals: ...

title: #product: 1768760902
preview:

Paula Mendes: From telemetry POV, I like it. Support keeps getting “I didn’t get the email / ho...
text:
Marissa Cole: Question for the group: should we add an “Email preferences” link directly in Console Settings (e.g., Settings → Notifications) as part of the lifecycle sprint? Right now it’s only in the emails themselves.

Paula Mendes: From telemetry POV, I like it. Support keeps getting “I didn’t get the email / how do I unsubscribe” and it’s awkward to tell them to find a footer link in an email they didn’t receive.

Ben Carter: +1. Also reduces risk w/ CAN-SPAM complaints if people can quickly opt out. But we need to be clear what we’re letting them control: onboarding lifecycle vs product/security.

Tyler Benson: Marketing ops angle: having a consistent preference center helps deliverability. If users can’t find unsub easily, they hit “Report spam” and that burns our domain.

Dylan Brooks: Implementation-wise, are we thinking a deep link to the existing preferences page (hosted so...
